subject: DevOps
title: The npm Event-Stream Attack (2018)
description: A short lesson to help you apply The npm Event-Stream Attack (2018).

hook.text: A popular npm package got a new maintainer. That maintainer injected crypto-stealing malware.

buildup.text: The original author was tired and handed off ownership. No vetting of the new maintainer.

discovery.text: Malware hid inside a dependency of a dependency. It targeted one specific Bitcoin wallet app.

twist.text: It took weeks for the community to find the attack. Obfuscated code buried layers deep.

climax.text: The attack exposed fragility in open-source trust. Millions of packages depend on volunteers.

punchline.text: Audit dependency changes. Open-source trust is fragile.

quiz.question: How was malware introduced into the event-stream package?
options: A new maintainer added a malicious dependency
An automated bot modified the source code
